{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents provide information about the respiratory drive and the role of carbon dioxide and oxygen in stimulating the respiratory center. Document [26] explains that peripheral and central chemoreceptors respond to changes in hydrogen ions (H+) and CO2 levels, which are indicators of acid-base balance. An increase in arterial CO2 levels stimulates the chemoreceptors, leading to an increase in respiratory drive. The documents do not mention that a decrease in arterial CO2 or an increase in arterial oxygen levels stimulate the respiratory center as effectively as an increase in CO2.", "answer_choice": "B"}
